items.title: TWDC carriage exhibit — proposed delta vs. April 24 base draft
items.summary: TWDC counsel returned three changes to the carriage exhibit overnight; two are cosmetic, one redefines how marquee events count toward the minimum window, which materially affects scheduling flexibility for EverPass.

items.title: NESN — pricing checkpoint follow-up summary
items.summary: NESN reaffirmed two-year flat preference and asked for a Friday one-pager on 3.25x flat vs. escalator alternatives; April 30 launch remains contingent on Gate 3 clearance.

items.title: Morning brief supplement — overnight Outlook + SharePoint signals
items.summary: Three new partner signals overnight worth adding to the morning view: NESN flagged a counter on pricing escalator, ESPN/TWDC sent revised carriage exhibit comments, and Spectrum ad-sales pushed the DAI one-pager request.

items.title: Spectrum partner-intel — last 14 days; deal dormant; one comms reset
items.summary: Spectrum's RSN (SportsNet) deal is the live thread; v2 term sheet went out 2026-02-07 and has gone unanswered for ~10 weeks; rate card still blank everywhere; the only signal in the last 14 days is a Taylor Holden status-check call on 2026-04-16 that got rescheduled to next morning. Nothing material has moved on economics, MFN, or MG; we still own the entire posture stance. One template hygiene issue (Red Sox/Bruins avails language copy-pasted from NESN) needs to be cleaned before any further S…
